title: Microstructure formulation techniques for botulinum toxin
abstract: The present invention relates to microstructure formulation techniques for botulinum toxin. Microstructures made according to the microstructure formulation techniques of the present invention release a low-concentration botulinum toxin slowly over a long period of time, and thus are expected to greatly contribute to the safe and convenient medical use of botulinum toxin.
claims: Claims ( 27 ) A coating composition, containing: a botulinum toxin; a thickener; and a stabilizer. The coating composition of claim 1, wherein the botulinum toxin is selected from the group consisting of botulinum toxin type A, B, C, D, E, F, and G. The coating composition of claim 2, wherein the botulinum toxin is botulinum toxin type A. The coating composition of claim 1, wherein the thickener is any one or more selected from the group consisting of carboxymethylcellulose sodium salt, sodium alginate, hyaluronic acid, methyl cellulose, hydroxyethyl cellulose, and polyvinyl pyrrolidone. The coating composition of claim 4, wherein the thickener is contained in an amount of 0.05-10 wt%. The coating composition of claim 1, wherein the stabilizer is any one or more selected from the group consisting of trehalose, methionine, sodium phosphate, and a mixture of human serum albumin and sodium chloride. The coating composition of claim 6, wherein the stabilizer is contained in an amount of 0.03-30 wt%. The coating composition of claim 1, wherein the coating composition is coated on a surface of a microstructure. The coating composition of claim 8, wherein the microstructure is a microneedle, a microblade, a microknife, a microfiber, a microspike, a microprobe, a microbarb, a microarray, or a microelectrode. The coating composition of claim 9, wherein the microstructure is a microneedle. A microstructure, comprising: a botulinum toxin; a thickener; and a stabilizer. The microstructure of claim 11, wherein the botulinum toxin is selected from the group consisting of botulinum toxin type A, B, C, D, E, F, and G. The microstructure of claim 12, wherein the botulinum toxin is botulinum toxin type A. The microstructure of claim 11, wherein the thickener is any one or more selected from the group consisting of carboxymethylcellulose sodium salt, sodium alginate, hyaluronic acid, methyl cellulose, hydroxyethyl cellulose, and polyvinyl pyrrolidone. The microstructure of claim 14, wherein the thickener is contained in an amount of 0.05-10 wt%. The microstructure of claim 11, wherein the stabilizer is any one or more selected from the group consisting of trehalose, methionine, sodium phosphate, and a mixture of human serum albumin and sodium chloride. The microstructure of claim 16, wherein the stabilizer is contained in an amount of 0.03-30 wt%. The microstructure of claim 11, wherein the microstructure is a microneedle, a microblade, a microknife, a microfiber, a microspike, a microprobe, a microbarb, a microarray, or a microelectrode. The microstructure of claim 18, wherein the microstructure is a microneedle. A method for preparing a coating composition, comprising a mixture of a botulinum toxin, a thickener and a stabilizer. The method of claim 20, wherein the botulinum toxin is botulinum toxin type A. The method of claim 20, wherein the thickener is any one or more selected from the group consisting of carboxymethylcellulose sodium salt, sodium alginate, hyaluronic acid, methyl cellulose, hydroxyethyl cellulose, and polyvinyl pyrrolidone. The method of claim 20, wherein the stabilizer is any one or more selected from the group consisting of trehalose, methionine, sodium phosphate, and a mixture of human serum albumin and sodium chloride. A method for making a microstructure, comprising a botulinum toxin, a thickener and a stabilizer addting to a metal or a polymer compound. The method of claim 24, wherein the botulinum toxin is botulinum toxin type A. The method of claim 24, wherein the thickener is any one or more selected from the group consisting of carboxymethylcellulose sodium salt, sodium alginate, hyaluronic acid, methyl cellulose, hydroxyethyl cellulose, and polyvinyl pyrrolidone. The method of claim 24, wherein the stabilizer is any one or more selected from the group consisting of trehalose, methionine, sodium phosphate, and a mixture of human serum albumin and sodium chloride.
